Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core

Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.asset

package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.asset
Asset loading and lifecycle for FlixelGDX.

FlixelAssetManager: Centralized asset system used by FlixelGDX. It wraps a libGDX AssetManager and provides consistent helpers for loading (FlixelAssetManager.load(FlixelSource), FlixelAssetManager.load(String), update), strict access (require), and lifecycle policy (wrapper pools, reference counts, and clearing non persistent assets on state switches).

Prefer FlixelAssetManager.load(FlixelSource) for explicit asset types. FlixelAssetManager.load(String) resolves the path by file extension using a per-manager registry (FlixelAssetManager.registerExtension(java.lang.String, java.util.function.Function<java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.asset.FlixelSource<?>>)).

Game code typically uses Flixel.assets rather than constructing a manager directly, but the manager is instantiable and extendable for advanced use cases.

FlixelAsset: Public contract for typed handles (refcount, persist, load/require). Pooled handles come from FlixelAssetManager.obtainTypedAsset(String, Class) as FlixelTypedAsset (implementation detail). FlixelGraphic and FlixelSound also implement FlixelAsset where applicable.

FlixelSource: Small interface that lets built in and user defined source objects expose a consistent (assetKey, type) contract for loading and requiring through the manager.

FlixelWrapperSource: Extends FlixelSource with a pooled wrapper type (e.g. FlixelGraphic) resolved via FlixelAssetManager.obtainWrapper(String, Class). Registering new wrapper types uses FlixelAssetManager.registerWrapperFactory(FlixelWrapperFactory); caller-owned wrappers use FlixelAssetManager.allocateSyntheticWrapperKey() and FlixelAssetManager.registerWrapper(FlixelPooledWrapper).

When to use sources vs wrappers

Choosing between extension loading and the wrapper pool
NeedUseWrapper factory?
Load a file as a libGDX type (texture, sound data, etc.) FlixelAssetManager.registerExtension(java.lang.String, java.util.function.Function<java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.asset.FlixelSource<?>>) + FlixelSource + load / get / source.require(assets) No, as it's not a pooled facade.
Refcount / persist / clear policy on a (key, Class) handle FlixelAssetManager.obtainTypedAsset(java.lang.String, java.lang.Class<T>) -> FlixelAsset No, as it's a typed handle, not the pooled wrapper system.
A second pooled object around the asset (policy, sharing), e.g. FlixelGraphic FlixelAssetManager.obtainWrapper(java.lang.String, java.lang.Class<W>); built-in FlixelGraphicWrapperFactory; FlixelAssetManager.registerWrapperFactory(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.asset.FlixelWrapperFactory<?>) only for new wrapper classes. Only if you introduce a new wrapper type beyond framework defaults.

Experts: Use FlixelAssetManager.getManager() only when you need raw libGDX behavior.

See Also:

    Package
    Description
    me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx
    The core package of the FlixelGDX framework.
  • Class
    Description
    FlixelAsset<T>
    Typed handle for one asset path + class, with optional persist and refcount.
    FlixelAssetManager
    Asset manager interface for FlixelGDX.
    FlixelDefaultAssetManager
    Default concrete asset manager for FlixelGDX.
    FlixelPooledWrapper
    Pooled wrapper registered through FlixelAssetManager (registerWrapper) for FlixelAssetManager.clearNonPersist() lifecycle.
    FlixelSource<T>
    Common interface for asset "source" objects.
    FlixelStringAssetLoader
    AssetManager loader that loads a text file into a String.
    FlixelStringAssetLoader.StringParameter
     
    FlixelStringAssetSource
    Source for plain text assets loaded via the libGDX String asset loader.
    FlixelTypedAsset<T>
    Default pooled implementation of FlixelAsset; constructed only via FlixelAssetManager.obtainTypedAsset(String, Class) or framework subclasses.
    FlixelWrapperFactory<W>
    Creates and pools wrapper instances for a single wrapper type.
    FlixelWrapperSource<T,W>
    An asset FlixelSource that also has a pooled Flixel wrapper type W (e.g.
